subConcepts.name: aspectuality
subConcepts.description: The property of representations whereby they always foreground a subset of features as relevant, presupposing rather than generating relevance.

keyPassages.phrase: a set of features that are relevant to each other and then a set of features that have been structurally functionally organised so that they have co-relevance, is then relevant to me. That's what an aspect is

subConcepts.name: combinatorial-explosion-constraint
subConcepts.description: The computational barrier that forces relevance realization: the space of possible inferences, features, or operations is too large to search exhaustively.

keyPassages.phrase: the number of implications, logical relations between any proposition and all the other propositions is combinatorially explosive. Combinatorially explosive! You cannot ever make use … of all of the implications of any proposition, ever

subConcepts.name: enactive-demonstrative-reference
subConcepts.description: The pre-categorical, pre-conceptual capacity to salience-tag a here-now locus in the world, which underlies all subsequent categorization.

keyPassages.phrase: I need demonstrative reference I need enactive demonstrative reference before I can do any categorisation. Look, if I'm going to categorize things I need to mentally group them together.

subConcepts.name: auto-poetic-grounding
subConcepts.description: Relevance realization must ultimately be grounded in constitutive goals internal to a self-organizing living system rather than in externally represented targets.

keyPassages.phrase: The goals that are the originating source of Relevance Realisation have to be internal to the Relevance Realisation process.

subConcepts.name: scale-invariant-self-organization
subConcepts.description: Relevance realization operates simultaneously at multiple levels (feature and gestalt) through self-organizing processes rather than from a single central executive.

keyPassages.phrase: Relevance Realisation has to be happening at multiple levels of cognition in a simultaneous, self-organizing fashion. That's why it can lead to insight.

subConcepts.name: meaning-relevance-agency-triangle
subConcepts.description: Meaning, relevance, and agency form a mutually grounding triangle: relevance is the sense of connectedness constitutive of meaning, and agency is the relational enactment of that connection.

keyPassages.phrase: there will be deep connections between meaning and relevance … There's deep connections between relevance and agency … And there's going to be deep connections … between meaning and agency

subConcepts.name: non-circular-explanation-constraint
subConcepts.description: Any adequate theory of relevance realization must not presuppose relevance in the mechanisms it invokes—otherwise it commits the homuncular fallacy.

keyPassages.phrase: whatever process or entity that I'm trying to use to explain relevance should not itself require relevance … I have to find processes that are themselves, not processes that realize relevance

commonConfusions: Relevance realization is not a conscious process of selecting information—it is a sub-representational, pre-categorical self-organizing process that underlies all conscious cognition.
Relevance is not a property of objects in the world nor a subjective projection from the mind—it is transjective, emerging in the co-created relationship between organism and environment.
Relevance realization cannot be explained by representations, computations, or a central executive because each of those already presupposes relevance to function.
Relevance realization is not a single mechanism located in one brain region but operates self-organizingly at multiple levels simultaneously.

keyPassages.phrase: you have to ignore the information, not even check it … See Relevance Realization isn't the application of a definition.
At the core of your ability to communicate is your ability to realize relevant information.
what you're now doing is you're moving from a logical to a psychological account of similarity

we cannot have a scientific theory of relevance … that tells us something very deep about the nature of relevance and therefore something deep about the nature of meaning … the fact that we don't have a theory of relevance doesn't preclude us from having a theory of Relevance Realisation

relevance realization is inherently interested in itself because, as I've argued, that's constitutive of it being a self-organizing, self-correcting, self-optimizing, self-developing, evolving process

subConcepts.name: frame-breaking
subConcepts.description: The first phase of insight in which an inappropriate representational frame (gestalt) is disrupted, de-automatizing cognition.

keyPassages.phrase: I have to break up an inappropriate frame. What do I have to do? I have to break up the gestalt. And I also have to de-automatize my cognition.

subConcepts.name: insight-cascade
subConcepts.description: The extended flow of successive insight events that characterizes deep learning—each restructuring primes the next.

keyPassages.phrase: That's the flow state. It's an insight cascade. So the more you flow, the more you're training your ability for insight.

subConcepts.name: transframing-insight
subConcepts.description: A systematic insight that does not merely reframe a single problem but transforms the competence that generates frames—characteristic of wisdom development.

keyPassages.phrase: it not only is reframing the problem. It is transforming my competence so it is a transframing insight. It is a systematic insight.

commonConfusions: Insight is not merely a lucky guess—it requires first breaking an inappropriate frame and then restructuring how features cohere.
Insight cannot be forced by deliberate logical search; the de-automatization that enables it often requires indirect practices like mindfulness or flow.
Insight into a single problem is distinct from the systematic, transframing insight associated with wisdom, which transforms whole competences rather than individual solutions.

keyPassages.phrase: I have to break up an inappropriate frame. What do I have to do? I have to break up the gestalt. And I also have to de-automatize my cognition.

Mindfulness is basically teaching us how to appropriate and train a flexibility of attentional scaling so that we can intervene effectively in how we are framing our problems and increase the chances of insight when insight is needed.
attention isn't a spotlight. It's a very complex optimisation process.
That's the flow state. It's an insight cascade. So the more you flow, the more you're training your ability for insight.
it not only is reframing the problem. It is transforming my competence so it is a transframing insight. It is a systematic insight.

subConcepts.name: skills-demands-matching
subConcepts.description: The environmental condition for flow: situational demands slightly exceed current skill level, requiring full engagement without overwhelming.

keyPassages.phrase: the flow state is one in which the demands of the situation just slightly go beyond your skill abilities … If my skills exceed the demands, I fall into boredom. If my demands exceed the skills, I fall into anxiety.

subConcepts.name: implicit-learning-enhancement
subConcepts.description: Flow's tight-coupled, error-sensitive feedback environment trains implicit learning to track causal rather than merely correlational patterns.

keyPassages.phrase: those three criteria that will turn your intuition into good implicit learning are exactly the conditions for flow. Clear information. Tightly coupled feedback. And error matters.

subConcepts.name: reduced-self-monitoring
subConcepts.description: In flow, autobiographical self-consciousness and image-management recede, freeing cognitive resources for the task.

keyPassages.phrase: when you're in the flow state, a kind of self-consciousness disappears … It's constantly doing our autobiography … All of that nattering … and that … can get out of hand … But in the flow state, it's gone

commonConfusions: Flow is not simply enjoyment or physical pleasure—it is an altered cognitive state characterized by an insight cascade and enhanced implicit learning.
Flow is not effortlessness in the ordinary sense; there is tremendous metabolic effort, yet the effort feels spontaneous and unforced.
Flow is not identical to mindfulness practice, though mindfulness training demonstrably increases the capacity for flow.

keyPassages.phrase: the flow state is one in which the demands of the situation just slightly go beyond your skill abilities
That's the flow state. It's an insight cascade. So the more you flow, the more you're training your ability for insight.
those three criteria that will turn your intuition into good implicit learning are exactly the conditions for flow. Clear information. Tightly coupled feedback. And error matters.
The more often you get into the flow state the more likely you will rate your life as meaningful. The more you will experience well-being.
We can address the reflectiveness gap by the cultivation of flow.

subConcepts.name: embodiment
subConcepts.description: Cognition is grounded in sensorimotor interaction with the world; mind arises through immediate coping, not rule-bound symbol manipulation.

keyPassages.phrase: Mind is not programmed software or rule-bound manipulation of symbols. Instead, the mind arises through immediate coping with the world.

subConcepts.name: embeddedness
subConcepts.description: Organisms and environments are co-constituting through niche construction; cognition cannot be understood apart from environmental coupling.

keyPassages.phrase: the organism is shaping the environment, shaping and selecting the environment, and the environment is shaping and selecting the organism … You've got this transjective evolving loop going on—niche construction.

subConcepts.name: enaction
subConcepts.description: Cognition is something organisms do—it is enacted through the sensorimotor loop—rather than a passive reception of inputs.

keyPassages.phrase: cognition is an inherently something we enact

subConcepts.name: extension
subConcepts.description: Cognition extends beyond the brain through psychotechnologies, artifacts, and distributed social networks.

keyPassages.phrase: our cognition is not in our head, but it's extended through our interactions, through the world, through our psychotechnologies, through distributed cognition

subConcepts.name: deep-continuity-hypothesis
subConcepts.description: There is continuity between the principles of cognition, biology, and self-organizing dynamical systems, placing mind within the natural order rather than above it.

keyPassages.phrase: the mind and the world are not disconnected. They are in a deep continuity of embeddedness and enactive processing.

commonConfusions: 4E cognition does not deny the importance of internal representations—it denies that representations are the origin or sufficient basis for cognition.
Embeddedness is not the same as mere environmental influence; it refers to the co-constituting loop between organism and environment in niche construction.
4E cognition is not anti-scientific—it is a third-generation cognitive science that expands the scientific account of mind beyond computational internalism.
The four E's are distinct emphases within a unified framework, not independent theories; they mutually presuppose the organism-environment loop.

keyPassages.phrase: Mind is not programmed software or rule-bound manipulation of symbols. Instead, the mind arises through immediate coping with the world.
our cognition is not in our head, but it's extended through our interactions, through the world, through our psychotechnologies, through distributed cognition
the mind and the world are not disconnected. They are in a deep continuity of embeddedness and enactive processing.
The world and the organism are co-creating co-determining co-evolving the fittedness.
your cognition is ultimately depended on, grounded in the relevance realization … and the relevance realization is ultimately grounded in your bioeconomy

subConcepts.name: foregrounding-backgrounding
subConcepts.description: Consciousness differentiates relevance topographically: some features are foregrounded (highly salient) and others recede into the background.

keyPassages.phrase: Part of what's happening, part of what consciousness is doing, is it's creating a salience landscape for me.

subConcepts.name: salience-tagging
subConcepts.description: The pre-categorical cognitive act of marking a here-now locus as salient, prior to identifying or categorizing what it is.

keyPassages.phrase: This ability to do this is like salience tagging … When I touch this I am making this here-nowness salient to me.

subConcepts.name: optimal-grip-coupling
subConcepts.description: The salience landscape is dynamically adjusted to achieve optimal grip—a balance of abstraction and detail suited to the current task.

keyPassages.phrase: I'm taking my salience landscape and I'm using it to get an optimal grip on things. Not maximal. And 'grip' is meant here as a metaphor.

commonConfusions: A salience landscape is not a fixed spotlight of attention; it is a dynamic topography that shifts as the agent-arena relationship evolves.
Salience is not purely subjective preference—it tracks real affordances in the environment co-created by organism and world.
The salience landscape is not reducible to explicit beliefs about what matters; it operates pre-reflectively and shapes what enters reflective cognition.

keyPassages.phrase: Part of what's happening, part of what consciousness is doing, is it's creating a salience landscape for me.
attention isn't a spotlight. It's a very complex optimisation process.
I'm taking my salience landscape and I'm using it to get an optimal grip on things. Not maximal. And 'grip' is meant here as a metaphor.
This ability to do this is like salience tagging … When I touch this I am making this here-nowness salient to me.
my perspectival knowing, it's your ability to do salience landscaping and it's ability to foreground, background, formulate the problem

subConcepts.name: self-organizing-self-destructive-loop
subConcepts.description: The core structure of parasitic processing: an adaptive self-organization that hijacks the agent's resources and progressively undermines the very machinery it depends on.

keyPassages.phrase: We called this in, when we published, 'Parasitic Processing'. It's not just about bad events. This is just one example. We get into all kinds of these spirals.

subConcepts.name: modal-confusion-as-driver
subConcepts.description: Trying to satisfy being-mode needs (love, connection) within the having-mode generates the misfit that feeds parasitic processing.

keyPassages.phrase: We can address modal confusion by the cultivation of Sati.

subConcepts.name: counteractive-dynamical-system
subConcepts.description: A set of interlocking practices (e.g., the Eightfold Path) designed as a dynamical counterforce to the self-organizing momentum of parasitic processing.

keyPassages.phrase: The Eightfold Path is a counteractive dynamical system that counteracts parasitic processing and does reciprocal opening, beyond the ego self and beyond the everyday world

commonConfusions: Parasitic processing is not simply bad habit—it is self-organizing and self-reinforcing, borrowing the adaptive machinery of relevance realization against the agent's interests.
The perennial problems Vervaeke identifies are not moral failures; they are structural vulnerabilities built into the very machinery that makes intelligent agency possible.
Addressing parasitic processing requires a counteractive dynamical system, not merely willpower or propositional insight, because the process operates below the level of deliberate control.

keyPassages.phrase: We called this in, when we published, 'Parasitic Processing'. It's not just about bad events. This is just one example. We get into all kinds of these spirals.

Marc proposes a model that he calls 'Reciprocal Narrowing'. So here's your Agent and here's the Arena (drawn on the board). And what happens is the drug use is associated with a particular Agent:Arena relationship.
The Eightfold Path is a counteractive dynamical system that counteracts parasitic processing and does reciprocal opening, beyond the ego self and beyond the everyday world
the very processes that make us intelligently adaptive also make us vulnerable to self-deceptive, self-destructive behavior
all cultures, across time, place, history … People are prone to perennial problems. These are ways in which the machinery of Relevance Realization can drive them into meaninglessness, despair.

subConcepts.name: having-mode
subConcepts.description: An existential orientation toward the world as a set of possessions or objects to be acquired and controlled.

keyPassages.phrase: Fromm's point is we get mixed up. We try to satisfy our being needs within the having mode. We suffer from 'Modal Confusion'.

subConcepts.name: being-mode
subConcepts.description: An existential orientation of participation, engagement, and identity-transformation rather than acquisition.

keyPassages.phrase: Fromm's point is we get mixed up. We try to satisfy our being needs within the having mode. We suffer from 'Modal Confusion'.

commonConfusions: Modal confusion is not simply a cognitive error about facts; it is an existential misalignment between the mode of engagement and the need being pursued.
Having-mode engagement is not inherently wrong—the problem arises only when being-mode needs are pursued through having-mode strategies.

keyPassages.phrase: Fromm's point is we get mixed up. We try to satisfy our being needs within the having mode. We suffer from 'Modal Confusion'.
We can address modal confusion by the cultivation of Sati.
all cultures, across time, place, history … People are prone to perennial problems. These are ways in which the machinery of Relevance Realization can drive them into meaninglessness, despair.
This process by which you are co-identifying agency and arena so that they fit together and make sense of each other and you get a coherent and functioning world view... That's your existential mode.
We get into all kinds of these spirals.

subConcepts.name: transformation-of-framing-process
subConcepts.description: Transframing operates on the meta-level: it alters not just a current frame but the whole framing process—both ends of the act of making sense.

keyPassages.phrase: It's not a reframing of a particular problem. It's a transformation of the whole framing process: Both ends.

subConcepts.name: existential-stupefaction-threshold
subConcepts.description: The condition that necessitates transframing: when one is stuck and unable to imagine how to make an alternative worldview significant.

keyPassages.phrase: They're stuck and they're stupefied. And when we're stuck and we're stupefied when we're stuck, we can't imagine how to make an alternative worldview significant.

subConcepts.name: gnosis-as-transframing-context
subConcepts.description: Gnosis is the psychotechnology that creates a ritual context enabling transframing—overcoming existential stuckness through participatory transformation.

keyPassages.phrase: Gnosis is to have a set of psychotechnologies that create a ritual context like Jeepform, like martial arts, like therapy that allows us to overcome being existentially stuck, existentially stupefied.

commonConfusions: Transframing is not ordinary reframing (finding a new perspective on an existing problem); it transforms the capacity that generates frames itself.
Transframing cannot be achieved through propositional argument alone because the problem it addresses is participatory and perspectival, not merely informational.
The systematic transframing of wisdom is not a single dramatic event but an ongoing developmental process of expanding competence.

keyPassages.phrase: It's not a reframing of a particular problem. It's a transformation of the whole framing process: Both ends.
They're stuck and they're stupefied. And when we're stuck and we're stupefied when we're stuck, we can't imagine how to make an alternative worldview significant.

Gnosis is to have a set of psychotechnologies that create a ritual context like Jeepform, like martial arts, like therapy that allows us to overcome being existentially stuck, existentially stupefied.
it not only is reframing the problem. It is transforming my competence so it is a transframing insight. It is a systematic insight.
Mindfulness is basically teaching us how to appropriate and train a flexibility of attentional scaling so that we can intervene effectively in how we are framing our problems

subConcepts.name: neither-projected-nor-detected
subConcepts.description: Transjective properties (e.g., relevance, affordances) are neither projected from the mind onto a neutral world nor passively detected in an independent world; they arise in the organism-environment relationship.

keyPassages.phrase: I want to propose a term to you. I want to argue that relevance is in this sense, transjective (writes this on the board). It is a real relationship between the organism and its environment. We should not think of it as being projected. We should not think of it as being detected.

subConcepts.name: co-evolutionary-fittedness
subConcepts.description: Organism and world co-create, co-determine, and co-evolve the fittedness that makes meaning possible.

keyPassages.phrase: The world and the organism are co-creating co-determining co-evolving the fittedness.

subConcepts.name: transjectivity-and-meaning
subConcepts.description: Meaning is not made by the subject nor found in the object but cultivated in the transjective relation—neither romantic imposition nor empiricist reception.

keyPassages.phrase: the transjectivity here—that it's actually in here, that the meaning is to be found and notice also the connectivity and how much of this is, you know, the relevance realizing, the deep connection, the deep caring, the deep involvement, the deep participation

commonConfusions: Transjective does not mean intersubjective (agreed upon by multiple subjects)—it refers to the real relational structure between organism and environment, independent of agreement.
Acknowledging that relevance is transjective is not a retreat to subjectivism; it is a claim that the relationship itself has real structure.
Transjectivity does not require any mystical or non-physical ontology—it is grounded in the biological and cognitive science of niche construction and enaction.

keyPassages.phrase: I want to propose a term to you. I want to argue that relevance is in this sense, transjective (writes this on the board). It is a real relationship between the organism and its environment. We should not think of it as being projected. We should not think of it as being detected.
The world and the organism are co-creating co-determining co-evolving the fittedness.
Is it therefore just pointing to something that's romantically dwelling within my subjectivity? No. It's pointing to caring about something that is inherently transjective
Relevance realization is not something in me. It's not something in the world. It emerges in the affordances that are co-created by the world in myself.
If the agent and arena need to be in a conformity relationship, they need to be well fitted together … in order for my salience landscaping to function appropriately.

subConcepts.name: modal-confusion-perennial
subConcepts.description: Confusing having-mode and being-mode strategies, leading to pursuing existential needs through possession and control.

keyPassages.phrase: We can address modal confusion by the cultivation of Sati.

subConcepts.name: reflectiveness-gap
subConcepts.description: The failure of second-order cognition to track first-order processes, generating self-opacity and self-deception.

keyPassages.phrase: We can address the reflectiveness gap by the cultivation of flow.

subConcepts.name: absurdity
subConcepts.description: The collision between the agent's implicit commitment to meaning and the explicit recognition of groundlessness, creating existential vertigo.

keyPassages.phrase: We can address absurdity by cultivating Prajñā.

subConcepts.name: anxiety
subConcepts.description: Pervasive existential threat-detection untethered from specific dangers, arising from the self-monitoring of a meaning-seeking being.

keyPassages.phrase: We can address anxiety by cultivating inner dialogue by internalizing the sage

subConcepts.name: alienation
subConcepts.description: The breakdown of communitas—feeling fundamentally separated from others and from the conditions that make shared meaning possible.

keyPassages.phrase: we can address the process of alienation through the cultivation of communitas

commonConfusions: The perennial problems are not unique to modernity—they are structural vulnerabilities of the relevance-realization machinery present across all cultures and times.
They are not moral failings or character defects; they arise precisely because the same machinery that makes us intelligently adaptive can be turned against itself.
Addressing the perennial problems requires a counteractive dynamical system of practices, not philosophical argument or willpower alone.
The perennial problems are distinct from each other and call for distinct countermeasures, though they interact and mutually reinforce one another.

keyPassages.phrase: all cultures, all people – all people in the sense of being participants in their culture, not necessarily every single individual – but all cultures, across time, place, history, right? People are prone to perennial problems. These are ways in which the machinery of Relevance Realization can drive them into meaninglessness, despair.
the very processes that make us intelligently adaptive also make us vulnerable to self-deceptive, self-destructive behavior

We can address parasitic processing with a counteractive dynamical system. We can address modal confusion by the cultivation of Sati. We can address the reflectiveness gap by the cultivation of flow. We can address absurdity by cultivating Prajñā.

I want to reverse-engineer enlightenment. I want to understand what the perennial problems are and using this machinery (indicates RR, Religio, Sacredness), what are the practices (indicates Wisdom, Enlightenment, Salvation), what are the processes we can use in order to address the perennial problems?
The Eightfold Path is a counteractive dynamical system that counteracts parasitic processing and does reciprocal opening, beyond the ego self and beyond the everyday world

subConcepts.name: ineffability
subConcepts.description: Higher states resist propositional capture; participants consistently describe them as beyond language and ordinary categorization.

keyPassages.phrase: I call this the problem of the 'Ontonormativity.' Remember that 'ontology' has to do with the structure of reality. Normative is when things are placing a demand on you to be better, to improve.

subConcepts.name: systematic-insight
subConcepts.description: Higher states afford insight not just into a particular problem but into the framing process itself—a transframing that transforms the capacity for relevance realization.

keyPassages.phrase: it not only is reframing the problem. It is transforming my competence so it is a transframing insight. It is a systematic insight.

subConcepts.name: gnosis-empowerment
subConcepts.description: Higher states of consciousness function as the engine that empowers the participatory transformation (gnosis) required to overcome existential entrapment.

keyPassages.phrase: we can respond to existential entrapment by the cultivation of gnosis, which can be empowered by a core capacity for realizing higher states of consciousness

commonConfusions: Higher states of consciousness are not merely more intense versions of normal experience—they involve a qualitative restructuring of the salience landscape and the sense of self.
They are not automatically veridical—their epistemic value depends on whether the insight they afford survives critical integration with waking cognition.
Higher states are not restricted to drug-induced experiences; flow, deep meditation, and ritual practice can all occasion them.

keyPassages.phrase: The core function of consciousness seems to be to help you realize, become aware of, actualize, put into action, 'Relevance': relevant information.
I call this the problem of the 'Ontonormativity.' Remember that 'ontology' has to do with the structure of reality. Normative is when things are placing a demand on you to be better, to improve.
we can respond to existential entrapment by the cultivation of gnosis, which can be empowered by a core capacity for realizing higher states of consciousness

people claim to have the most profound sense of meaning and it is precisely ineffable. They reliably, across traditions, across historical contexts, claim that it is not conceptual, it can't be grasped categorically and they use the language of hereness and nowness to describe it

Getting into the flow state is deeply deeply enhancing of your cognition … what's happening in this state is your brain is learning to get areas to talk to each other that normally don't talk to each other.

subConcepts.name: disruptive-strategies
subConcepts.description: Deliberate practices (sleep deprivation, fasting, psychedelics, chanting) that destabilize default framing to enable cognitive exaptation.

keyPassages.phrase: typically the shaman engages in practices that are putting significant changes in their attention … disruptive strategies: sleep deprivation, sex depravation, social isolation, the use of psychedelics, extended chanting, dancing. All of these things are designed to bring about radical changes in the way in which the brain is operating.

subConcepts.name: cross-neural-integration
subConcepts.description: Altered states permit brain areas that do not normally communicate to interact, enabling novel insight and pattern bridging.

keyPassages.phrase: what's happening in this state is your brain is learning to get areas to talk to each other that normally don't talk to each other. This is especially the case if you've gone through a massive disruption strategy

subConcepts.name: cognitive-exaptation
subConcepts.description: Using capacities evolved for one function (e.g., sensorimotor pattern recognition) in radically new cognitive domains via altered states and psychotechnology.

keyPassages.phrase: the Upper Paleolithic transition was probably driven by the way shamanism was a set of pscyho-technologies for altering states of consciousness to cognitively exapt the enhanced abilities

commonConfusions: Altered states are not simply confusion or impairment—they can afford genuine cognitive enhancement when embedded in appropriate practice contexts.
The value of altered states depends entirely on what is done with the cross-neural integration they make available; insight must be integrated into waking cognition.
Altered states are not the same as higher states of consciousness; altered states are the vehicle, higher states are the destination—though they often overlap.

keyPassages.phrase: the Upper Paleolithic transition was probably driven by the way shamanism was a set of pscyho-technologies for altering states of consciousness to cognitively exapt the enhanced abilities
what's happening in this state is your brain is learning to get areas to talk to each other that normally don't talk to each other.
Getting into the flow state: Notice what's going on here. Notice you're getting something that's almost like a mystical experience. It's a powerful, altered state of consciousness.
we can respond to existential entrapment by the cultivation of gnosis, which can be empowered by a core capacity for realizing higher states of consciousness
The core function of consciousness seems to be to help you realize, become aware of, actualize, put into action, 'Relevance': relevant information.

subConcepts.name: co-identification
subConcepts.description: The mutual shaping of agent and arena such that a coherent identity on each side emerges simultaneously from their fit together.

keyPassages.phrase: This process by which you are co-identifying agency and arena so that they fit together and make sense of each other and you get a coherent and functioning world view... That's your existential mode.

subConcepts.name: worldview-coupling
subConcepts.description: The agent-arena relationship is the structural unit of a worldview: having a worldview just is having this coupling operational.

keyPassages.phrase: what you have, when you have a world view, is you've got this Agent and Arena coupling.

commonConfusions: The agent-arena relationship is not merely a context or background—it is the primary unit of meaning-making from which all more specific projects derive.
Arena does not mean the physical environment alone; it includes the normative and narrative orders that make the environment intelligible to an agent.

keyPassages.phrase: what you have, when you have a world view, is you've got this Agent and Arena coupling.
This process by which you are co-identifying agency and arena so that they fit together and make sense of each other and you get a coherent and functioning world view... That's your existential mode.
The world and the organism are co-creating co-determining co-evolving the fittedness.
the agent and arena need to be in a conformity relationship, they need to be well fitted together … in order for my salience landscaping to function appropriately
there will be deep connections between meaning and relevance … There's deep connections between relevance and agency … there's going to be deep connections … between meaning and agency

subConcepts.name: relevance-realization-function
subConcepts.description: The primary cognitive function of consciousness is to enact relevance realization—making relevant information available for adaptive action.

keyPassages.phrase: The core function of consciousness seems to be to help you realize, become aware of, actualize, put into action, 'Relevance': relevant information.

subConcepts.name: ontonormativity
subConcepts.description: The normative demand built into conscious experience: the sense that reality is placing a call on the agent to orient correctly, to improve one's grip on what is real.

keyPassages.phrase: I call this the problem of the 'Ontonormativity.' Remember that 'ontology' has to do with the structure of reality. Normative is when things are placing a demand on you to be better, to improve.

subConcepts.name: working-memory-as-relevance-filter
subConcepts.description: Working memory functions as a relevance filter, screening off irrelevant information and allowing deeper processing of what matters—and is correlated with general intelligence.

keyPassages.phrase: the job of working memory is to be a relevance filter and to screen off irrelevant information and allow in to processing, deeper processing, more relevant information

commonConfusions: Consciousness is not merely the passive theatre in which mental content plays out—its core function is the active enactment of relevance realization.
Ontonormativity is not a metaphysical claim about objective values; it describes the phenomenology of consciousness placing normative demands on cognition.
The hard problem of consciousness (why there is phenomenal experience) is distinct from the functional question (what consciousness does) that Vervaeke primarily addresses.

keyPassages.phrase: The core function of consciousness seems to be to help you realize, become aware of, actualize, put into action, 'Relevance': relevant information.
Part of what's happening, part of what consciousness is doing, is it's creating a salience landscape for me.
I call this the problem of the 'Ontonormativity.' Remember that 'ontology' has to do with the structure of reality. Normative is when things are placing a demand on you to be better, to improve.
consciousness seems to be bound up with Relevance Realisation
the job of working memory is to be a relevance filter and to screen off irrelevant information and allow in to processing, deeper processing, more relevant information

commonConfusions: Optimal grip is not maximal grip—seeking maximum detail or maximum abstraction both impede the dynamic balance that characterizes good cognition.
Optimal grip is not a fixed setting; it must be continuously adjusted as the agent-arena relationship evolves.
Optimal grip is not purely cognitive; it is enacted through the body and the sensorimotor loop, not calculated abstractly.

keyPassages.phrase: I'm taking my salience landscape and I'm using it to get an optimal grip on things. Not maximal. And 'grip' is meant here as a metaphor.
attention isn't a spotlight. It's a very complex optimisation process.
my perspectival knowing, it's your ability to do salience landscaping and it's ability to foreground, background, formulate the problem
The world and the organism are co-creating co-determining co-evolving the fittedness.
relevance realization is inherently interested in itself because … that's constitutive of it being a self-organizing, self-correcting, self-optimizing, self-developing, evolving process

commonConfusions: Ontonormativity is not a metaphysical claim that values are built into mind-independent reality; it is a phenomenological claim about how consciousness presents normative demands.
It is not identical to moral normativity; it is the broader sense in which cognition is pulled toward better contact with what is real.
Ontonormativity is not a problem to be solved but a structural feature of consciousness that must be acknowledged in any adequate cognitive science of mind.

keyPassages.phrase: I call this the problem of the 'Ontonormativity.' Remember that 'ontology' has to do with the structure of reality. Normative is when things are placing a demand on you to be better, to improve.
The core function of consciousness seems to be to help you realize, become aware of, actualize, put into action, 'Relevance': relevant information.
consciousness seems to be bound up with Relevance Realisation
Is it therefore just pointing to something that's romantically dwelling within my subjectivity? No. It's pointing to caring about something that is inherently transjective
wisdom has to do with these things … understanding … grasping the significance of the facts that you have

subConcepts.name: attentional-scaling
subConcepts.description: The dynamic adjustment of attention between fine-grained features and coarser gestalt patterns—a skill trainable through mindfulness.

keyPassages.phrase: Mindfulness is basically teaching us how to appropriate and train a flexibility of attentional scaling so that we can intervene effectively in how we are framing our problems

commonConfusions: Attention is not a spotlight that selects from a neutral field; it is a complex optimization process co-constituting what counts as relevant.
Training attention (e.g., through mindfulness) is not the same as training concentration; it is training the flexibility to shift between scales of relevance.

keyPassages.phrase: attention isn't a spotlight. It's a very complex optimisation process.

Mindfulness is basically teaching us how to appropriate and train a flexibility of attentional scaling so that we can intervene effectively in how we are framing our problems and increase the chances of insight when insight is needed.

you know that you're always going from feature to gestalt and from gestalt to feature … Relevance Realisation has to be happening both at the features level and the gestalt level in a highly integrated, interactive fashion.
my perspectival knowing, it's your ability to do salience landscaping and it's ability to foreground, background, formulate the problem
Part of what's happening, part of what consciousness is doing, is it's creating a salience landscape for me.

commonConfusions: Existential mode is not a belief or theory about oneself; it is a pre-reflective orientation enacted in the agent-arena coupling.
Switching existential modes is not a mere change of attitude—it requires transforming the participatory knowing that grounds the agent's world.

keyPassages.phrase: This process by which you are co-identifying agency and arena so that they fit together and make sense of each other and you get a coherent and functioning world view... That's your existential mode.
Fromm's point is we get mixed up. We try to satisfy our being needs within the having mode. We suffer from 'Modal Confusion'.
what you have, when you have a world view, is you've got this Agent and Arena coupling.
We can address modal confusion by the cultivation of Sati.
The world and the organism are co-creating co-determining co-evolving the fittedness.

commonConfusions: Worldview attunement is not the same as having correct beliefs about the world; it is the participatory fit between agent and arena that makes any belief formation possible.
Loss of worldview attunement is not merely confusion—it is an existential crisis in which the machinery of meaning generation itself breaks down.

keyPassages.phrase: what you have, when you have a world view, is you've got this Agent and Arena coupling.
This process by which you are co-identifying agency and arena so that they fit together and make sense of each other and you get a coherent and functioning world view... That's your existential mode.
having a ecology of psychotechnologies for addressing the perennial problems is necessary, but, of course, not sufficient for responding to the meaning crisis
whatever machinery we craft together for addressing the perennial problems has to be integrated, grounded in an agapic way of being
the perspectival ultimately depends on the participatory

commonConfusions: Existential entrapment is not ordinary indecision or laziness; it is a cognitive-existential paralysis in which the agent cannot imaginatively inhabit an alternative worldview.
Overcoming existential entrapment requires gnosis (participatory transformation), not propositional argument—being told a better worldview exists does not liberate a trapped agent.

keyPassages.phrase: They're stuck and they're stupefied. And when we're stuck and we're stupefied when we're stuck, we can't imagine how to make an alternative worldview significant.

Gnosis is to have a set of psychotechnologies that create a ritual context like Jeepform, like martial arts, like therapy that allows us to overcome being existentially stuck, existentially stupefied.
we can respond to existential entrapment by the cultivation of gnosis, which can be empowered by a core capacity for realizing higher states of consciousness
It's not a reframing of a particular problem. It's a transformation of the whole framing process: Both ends.
all cultures, across time, place, history … People are prone to perennial problems. These are ways in which the machinery of Relevance Realization can drive them into meaninglessness, despair.

commonConfusions: Reciprocal narrowing is not merely reduced flexibility—both agent identity and arena narrow together, each change reinforcing the other in a self-sustaining spiral.
The mechanism is not specific to addiction; any parasitic processing that co-narrows agent and arena exemplifies reciprocal narrowing.

keyPassages.phrase: Marc proposes a model that he calls 'Reciprocal Narrowing'. So here's your Agent and here's the Arena (drawn on the board). And what happens is the drug use is associated with a particular Agent:Arena relationship.
We called this in, when we published, 'Parasitic Processing'. It's not just about bad events. This is just one example. We get into all kinds of these spirals.
The Eightfold Path is a counteractive dynamical system that counteracts parasitic processing and does reciprocal opening, beyond the ego self and beyond the everyday world
the very processes that make us intelligently adaptive also make us vulnerable to self-deceptive, self-destructive behavior
The world and the organism are co-creating co-determining co-evolving the fittedness.

commonConfusions: The divided psyche is not a clinical disorder but a structural feature of self-organizing minds whose sub-systems can develop competing relevance profiles.
Inner conflict arising from the divided psyche cannot be resolved by suppressing one side; it requires integrating the competing relevance patterns.

keyPassages.phrase: We get into all kinds of these spirals.
Fromm's point is we get mixed up. We try to satisfy our being needs within the having mode. We suffer from 'Modal Confusion'.
I have to break up an inappropriate frame. What do I have to do? I have to break up the gestalt. And I also have to de-automatize my cognition.
the very processes that make us intelligently adaptive also make us vulnerable to self-deceptive, self-destructive behavior
at the core of wisdom is the capacity for overcoming self-deception

commonConfusions: Inner conflict is not simply wanting two incompatible things; it arises from competing relevance-realization processes pulling the agent toward different agent-arena configurations.
Resolving inner conflict is not a matter of rational deliberation alone—it requires addressing the participatory and perspectival sources of the conflict.

keyPassages.phrase: We called this in, when we published, 'Parasitic Processing'. It's not just about bad events. This is just one example. We get into all kinds of these spirals.
the very processes that make us intelligently adaptive also make us vulnerable to self-deceptive, self-destructive behavior
Fromm's point is we get mixed up. We try to satisfy our being needs within the having mode. We suffer from 'Modal Confusion'.
at the core of wisdom is the capacity for overcoming self-deception
I have to break up an inappropriate frame. What do I have to do? I have to break up the gestalt. And I also have to de-automatize my cognition.

commonConfusions: Self-deception in Vervaeke's sense is not conscious lying to oneself; it operates through the sub-representational machinery of relevance realization and salience.
Overcoming self-deception is not a single act of will but requires systematic rationality—ongoing processes that reduce bias across multiple independent channels.

keyPassages.phrase: If you aren't clear about what this word means, how you're using this concept, you will be bullshitting yourself through equivocation
at the core of wisdom is the capacity for overcoming self-deception
what I'm going to mean by rationality is reliably and systematically … Overcoming self-deception
We can't just integrate by equivocating - no integration through equivocation because that's just bullshitting.
the very processes that make us intelligently adaptive also make us vulnerable to self-deceptive, self-destructive behavior

commonConfusions: Bullshit (in Frankfurt's and Vervaeke's sense) is not lying—the liar cares about truth and tries to hide it; the bullshitter is indifferent to truth and driven by salience or persuasion.
Bullshitting yourself through equivocation is not a deliberate strategy; it arises when salience overrides semantic vigilance.

keyPassages.phrase: If you aren't clear about what this word means, how you're using this concept, you will be bullshitting yourself through equivocation.
We can't just integrate by equivocating - no integration through equivocation because that's just bullshitting.

you know what I should do! You should eat a peanut butter and jelly sandwich and then commit suicide!! … that's ridiculous … the argument hinges on … using the same term … but not meaning the same thing
Cognitive Science is placed to address that by its very nature … it deals with … our propensity for bullshitting ourselves and deceiving ourselves.
conspiracy theories … They're a form of bullshitting because they're very: Look, if you will just accept … you can explain so much … Just give me this idea … and look at what I can do!

commonConfusions: Second-order thinking is not meta-cognition in the sense of reflecting on isolated beliefs; it involves attending to the processing that generates beliefs and frames, not just the products.
A rational person is not someone who is good at first-order logic; rationality centrally involves second-order attention to one's own cognitive processing.

keyPassages.phrase: a rational person is not only fixated on the products of their cognition, they pay attention to and find value in the processing of their cognition. That's what it is to be rational.
wisdom has a lot more to do with the process than with the product. Knowledge is often the product … but wisdom is, 'How am I knowing?'
If you don't get sensitive to the meaning of terms, you'll fall into equivocation, which is a disastrous way to try and reason about anything.
the propositional actually depends on the procedural … and the procedural knowing depends on your perspectival knowing … the perspectival ultimately depends on the participatory
overcoming self-deception in a reliable manner … reliably and systematically … Overcoming self-deception … afforded by some process of optimization of your cognitive processing

commonConfusions: Structural-functional organization is not a mere list of features; it is the coherent pattern in which features make each other relevant and together point toward a use or identity.
The same physical object can have different structural-functional organizations depending on the agent's context and projects—aspectuality is not fixed.

keyPassages.phrase: Are they just a feature list? No … they have a structural functional organization, they are made relevant to each other.

a set of features that are relevant to each other and then a set of features that have been structurally functionally organised so that they have co-relevance, is then relevant to me. That's what an aspect is

I hold this thing up (pen) and you form a representation of it … out of all the properties you just select some subset, and what subsets do you pick? Well, you pick a subset that is, here it comes, relevant to you!
Cognitive science is trying to create constructs that are multi-apt, that bridge between multiple domains, multiple disciplines.
Representations are aspectual … you're zeroing in on relevant properties out of all the possible properties

commonConfusions: Distributed cognition is not merely outsourcing memory to notebooks—it refers to the genuine extension of cognitive processing into tools, bodies, and social systems.
Distributed cognition does not eliminate individual cognitive responsibility; it changes the unit of cognitive analysis from brain to person-plus-environment system.

keyPassages.phrase: our cognition is not in our head, but it's extended through our interactions, through the world, through our psychotechnologies, through distributed cognition
culture is about both fitting people to an environment and fitting the environment to the people in this ongoing fashion

the organism is shaping the environment, shaping and selecting the environment, and the environment is shaping and selecting the organism … You've got this transjective evolving loop going on—niche construction.
The world and the organism are co-creating co-determining co-evolving the fittedness.
the perspectival ultimately depends on the participatory

commonConfusions: Implicit learning is not the same as unconscious bias—it is a genuine pattern-tracking capacity that can pick up on real causal structures, not only correlational artifacts.
Intuition produced by implicit learning is not magical; it is the surface expression of complex pattern recognition that cannot be fully articulated.

Explicit instruction does not improve implicit learning—it interferes with it; the relevant intervention is arranging the right environmental conditions (clear feedback, tight coupling, consequential error).

keyPassages.phrase: you have this tremendous capacity outside your conscious awareness - to pick up on very complex patterns in your environment. You say 'OK, why? What does this have to do with shamanism?'
those three criteria that will turn your intuition into good implicit learning are exactly the conditions for flow. Clear information. Tightly coupled feedback. And error matters.
if you put people into that experiment … and you tell them explicitly what they're supposed to do … their performance doesn't get better it gets worse
what we call intuition is a real thing but there isn't anything magical about it … your intuition is the result of your implicit learning
The biggot has got intuitions about races that are wrong … implicit learning goes wrong … it picks up on correlations [rather than causal patterns]

commonConfusions: Hyperbolic discounting is not irrational laziness; it is a structural bias in the temporal weighting of value built into the relevance-realization machinery.
It is not fixed or immutable—practices that cultivate expanded temporal horizons (such as wisdom cultivation) can partially counteract hyperbolic discounting.

keyPassages.phrase: all cultures, across time, place, history … People are prone to perennial problems. These are ways in which the machinery of Relevance Realization can drive them into meaninglessness, despair.
We get into all kinds of these spirals.
the very processes that make us intelligently adaptive also make us vulnerable to self-deceptive, self-destructive behavior
what I'm going to mean by rationality is reliably and systematically … Overcoming self-deception … afforded by some process of optimization of your cognitive processing
which beliefs do I need to change 'in this context'? … what makes somebody rational … is that you select out of all the possible implications, the relevant ones

commonConfusions: The frame problem is not merely a technical challenge for robotics; it reveals that relevance realization is irreducible to any formal algorithm.
Solving the frame problem does not mean specifying all relevant side effects—it means having a system that appropriately ignores most of them without checking them.

keyPassages.phrase: you have to ignore the information, not even check it … See Relevance Realization isn't the application of a definition.

You can't ignore all side effects or you'll be grotesquely stupid. You can't check all side effects or you'll be grotesquely incapable, and so therefore you have to zoom in on their relevant side effects.
whatever process or entity that I'm trying to use to explain relevance should not itself require relevance … I have to find processes that are themselves, not processes that realize relevance
Cognitive Science is placed to address that by its very nature … it deals with … our propensity for bullshitting ourselves and deceiving ourselves.

the number of implications, logical relations between any proposition and all the other propositions is combinatorially explosive … you cannot ever … make use of all of the implications of any proposition
